name: Primitive SOP
description: The Primitive SOP is like the Point SOP but manipulates a primitive's position, size, orientation, color, alpha, in addition to primitive-specific attributes, such as reversing primitive normals. The Primitive SOP also lets you create custom primitive attributes.
summary: The Primitive SOP is like the Point SOP but manipulates a primitive's position, size, orientation, color, alpha, in addition to primitive-specific attributes, such as reversing primitive normals. The

parameters.name: Source Group
parameters.label: Source Group
parameters.description: Parameter of this operator. See operator description for details.

parameters.name: Template Group
parameters.label: Template Group
parameters.description: templategrp - A subset of template points to transform to.

parameters.name: Do Transformation
parameters.label: Do Transformation
parameters.description: doxform - When checked, allows transformations to occur.

parameters.name: Rotate to Template
parameters.label: Rotate to Template
parameters.description:
dorot -  - A template can be specified using the second input of the  . When set to On, this template can be used to transform each primitive to the location and orientation of the template point. This is similar to what the Copy SOP does except that the actual primitives are transformed, not copies made.

 off - Don't rotate. on - The primitive gets rotated as if its normal was (0,0,1), and is meant to point the same direction as the template normal. match - Rotates the primitive so that its real normal lines up with the normal of the template point.

parameters.name: Off
parameters.label: Off
parameters.description: off - Don't rotate. on - The primitive gets rotated as if its normal was (0,0,1), and is meant to point the same direction as the template normal. match - Rotates the primitive so that its real normal lines up with the normal of the template point.

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description:
xord -  - Sets the overall transform order for the transformations. The transform order determines the order in which transformations take place. Depending on the order, you can achieve different results using the exact same values. Choose the appropriate order from the menu.

 srt - str - rst - rts - tsr - trs -

parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description:
rord -  - Sets the order of the rotations within the overall transform order.

 xyz - xzy - yxz - yzx - zxy - zyx -

parameters.description:
t -  - These three fields move the input geometry in the three axes. Profiles use tx and ty only.

p -  - The pivot point for the transformations. Profiles use px and py only.

parameters.name: Lookat Object
parameters.label: Lookat Object
parameters.description:
lookat - Selects the object the primitive should point towards. This performs the lookat in object space; if you need to a lookat in world space, use the lookat in the object's Transform page instead.	
Tip: In order to get multiple sprites to always be perpendicular to the camera, feed them into a   and specify the camera as your Lookat . Then the sprites should always be perpendicular to the camera.

parameters.name: Up-Vector
parameters.label: Up-Vector
parameters.description:
upvector -  - Defines the orientation of the primitive along the X, Y, or Z axes.	
The Up Vector determines how a primitive orients itself with respect to the target object (specified in Lookat ). The default value is of 1 in the Y direction. This will produce nice behaviour if you want the object to rotate somewhat in the Z axis as the Lookat Object gets very close and/or passes the target. Scaling the Up Vector will cause the Lookat primitives to remain more upright as they get very close and/or pass the target. The stronger the Up Vector, the more the primitives will want to stay vertical and resist the rotation.


 upvectorx - upvectory - upvectorz -

parameters.name: Color
parameters.label: Color
parameters.description:
doclr -  - If Keep is selected, the color attribute is left unchanged. If Add is selected, this parameter changes the color of input primitives according to diffuse color field. If No is selected, the color attribute is removed.

 off - on - remove -

parameters.name: Preserve Shape U
parameters.label: Preserve Shape U
parameters.description:
pshapeu - These options only become available once a type of clamping or closure has been selected.	
Closure - Change the closure and clamping of a face or hull.

parameters.name: Preserve Shape V
parameters.label: Preserve Shape V
parameters.description:
pshapev - These options only become available once a type of clamping or closure has been selected.	
Closure - Change the closure and clamping of a face or hull. The options are:

parameters.name: Close U
parameters.label: Close U
parameters.description:
closeu -  - Close the primitive in U / V. Select from: Open, Closed Straight, Close Rounded, and Unroll. When you unroll a closed curve you duplicate the wrapped points (you make them unique) and turn the curve into an open curve. The shape of the curve does not change. Same goes for hulls, only there we unique entire rows and cols. This addresses a problem with texturing wrapped surfaces whereby the texture repeats itself in the wrapped portion of the surface.

 sameclosure - open - closesharp - closeround - unroll -

parameters.name: No change
parameters.label: No change
parameters.description: sameclosure - open - closesharp - closeround - unroll -

parameters.name: Close V
parameters.label: Close V
parameters.description:
closev -  - Close the primitive in U / V. Select from: Open, Closed Straight, Close Rounded, and Unroll. When you unroll a closed curve you duplicate the wrapped points (you make them unique) and turn the curve into an open curve. The shape of the curve does not change. Same goes for hulls, only there we unique entire rows and cols. This addresses a problem with texturing wrapped surfaces whereby the texture repeats itself in the wrapped portion of the surface.

 sameclosure - open - closesharp - closeround - unroll -

parameters.name: Clamp U
parameters.label: Clamp U
parameters.description:
clampu -  - Clamp the primitive in U / V. Select from: Clamp, Unclamp.

 sameclamp - clamp - unclamp -

parameters.name: Clamp V
parameters.label: Clamp V
parameters.description:
clampv -  - Clamp the primitive in U / V. Select from: Clamp, Unclamp.

 sameclamp - clamp - unclamp -

parameters.name: Vertex
parameters.label: Vertex
parameters.description:
vtxsort -  - Reorder the vertices in a number of ways.

 samevertex - Does not affect the ordering of the vertices. reverse - Reverses both U and V for hulls, and just U for faces.For example: reverseu - Reverses column order of hulls. reversev - Reverses row order of hulls. swapuv - Interchanges rows/columns while preserving topology. shift - Cycles the vertices by "U Offset" and "V Offset". flipfacing -

parameters.name: U Offset
parameters.label: U Offset
parameters.description: vtxuoff - Cycles face or hull columns / rows when the Shift operation is selected.

parameters.name: V Offset
parameters.label: V Offset
parameters.description: vtxvoff - Cycles face or hull columns / rows when the Shift operation is selected.

parameters.name: Meta-Surface Weight
parameters.label: Meta-Surface Weight
parameters.description: metaweight - When selected, allows meta-surface weighting.

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.label: Weight
parameters.description: doweight - Enter weight of meta-surface here when Meta-surface Weight is selected.

parameters.name: Particle Render Type
parameters.label: Particle Render Type
parameters.description: doprender - When On the Particle Type menu below allows section of particle render type.

parameters.name: Particle Type
parameters.label: Particle Type
parameters.description:
prtype -  - Selects how the particles are rendered.

 lines - Each particle will be rendered as a 2-point line, with the length determined based on the particles velocity. If the particle has no velocity it will just be a single pixel in size. pointprites - For use with the Point Sprite MAT. Each particle is a square of pixels that always face the camera. The size of the square is determined by parameters in the  Sprite and the pscale vertex/point attribute. The point sprites will have texture coordinates generated for them automatically also ((0,0) in the bottom left and (1,1) in the top right). pointsprites -

parameters.name: Render as Lines
parameters.label: Render as Lines
parameters.description: lines - Each particle will be rendered as a 2-point line, with the length determined based on the particles velocity. If the particle has no velocity it will just be a single pixel in size. pointprites - For use with the Point Sprite MAT. Each particle is a square of pixels that always face the camera. The size of the square is determined by parameters in the  Sprite and the pscale vertex/point attribute. The point sprites will have texture coordinates generated for them automatically also ((0,0) in the bottom left and (1,1) in the top right). pointsprites -
